data.title: Earnings snapshot: Kroger Q1 earnings beats estimates; revenue misses; updates Identical sales without fuel outlook
data.description: *Other Operating Data Consensus Source: Bloomberg

data.title: Aflac reports cybersecurity incident
data.description:
Health insurer Aflac (NYSE:AFL) announced on Friday the identification of "suspicious activity" in its U.S. network on June 12.

"We promptly initiated our cyber incident response protocols and stopped the intrusion within hours," said the company in a statement.

Aflac said the investigation remains in its early stages, and preliminary findings indicate that the unauthorized party used social engineering tactics to gain access to the company's network. The insurer has commenced a review of the potentially impacted files.

The company said it is unable to determine the total number of affected individuals until the review is completed.

The insurer said the cybersecurity attack "was caused by a sophisticated cybercrime group", and "was part of a cybercrime campaign against the insurance industry".

data.title: Bakkt rises on $25M securities purchase agreement news
data.description:
Bakkt Holdings (NYSE:BKKT) was rising as the crypto solutions provider announced a $25M securities purchase agreement with the Cayman Islands exempt limited company, YA II PN.

Shares were +4.89% Friday pre-market to $13.31.

The investor YA II PN is set to acquire convertible debenture from Bakkt for $23.75M as part of the private placement. The private placement closed on June 18.

The convertible debenture, expected to mature on the first anniversary of the closing date, accrues interest at an annual rate equal to 0%, which can increase to a rate of 18% upon the occurrence of default.

The net proceeds are intended to be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: American Express, Dell, Walt Disney, Philip Morris, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from First Farmers Financial (OTCQX:FFMR) and Acme United (ACU) as well as declarations from companies such as American Express (NYSE:AXP), and Dell (NYSE:DELL).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Walt Disney (DIS) and Philip Morris (PM) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

First Farmers Financial (OTCQX:FFMR)

  • Dividend declared: June 17
  • New dividend amount: $0.50
  • Previous amount: $0.49
  • Increase: 2%

Acme United (ACU)

  • Dividend declared: June 18
  • New dividend amount: $0.16
  • Previous amount: $0.15
  • Increase: 6.7%

CNB Community Bancorp (OTCQX:CNBB)

  • Dividend declared: June 18
  • New dividend amount: $0.31
  • Previous amount: $0.30
  • Increase: 3.3%

Dividend Declarations

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend in the past week:

American Express (NYSE:AXP)

  • Dividend declared: June 17
  • Dividend amount: $0.82

Dell (NYSE:DELL)

  • Dividend declared: June 17
  • Dividend amount: $0.525

Bristol-Myers Squibb Company (BMY)

  • Dividend declared: June 17
  • Dividend amount: $0.62

U.S. Bancorp (USB)

  • Dividend declared: June 18
  • Dividend amount: $0.50

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Walt Disney (DIS)

  • Ex-dividend date: June 24
  • Payout date: July 23

Humana (HUM)

  • Ex-dividend date: June 27
  • Payout date: July 25

Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP)

  • Ex-dividend date: June 27
  • Payout date: July 11

Philip Morris (PM)

  • Ex-dividend date: June 27
  • Payout date: July 15

data.title: Kroger Non-GAAP EPS of $1.49 beats by $0.03, revenue of $45.12B misses by $190M
data.description:
• Kroger press release (NYSE:KR): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.49 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $45.12B (-0.4% Y/Y) misses by $190M.
  • Total company sales were $45.1 billion in the first quarter compared to $45.3 billion for the same period last year, which included $917 million from Kroger Specialty Pharmacy sales. Excluding fuel, Kroger Specialty Pharmacy and adjustment items, sales increased 3.7% compared to the same period last year.
  • eCommerce sales increased 15%
  • Identical Sales without fuel increased 3.2%

  • Full-Year 2025 Guidance*

    Updated

    • Identical Sales without fuel of 2.25% – 3.25%

    Reaffirmed

    • Adjusted FIFO Operating Profit of $4.7 – $4.9 billion
    • Adjusted net earnings per diluted share of $4.60 – $4.80
    • Adjusted Free Cash Flow of $2.8 – $3.0 billion
    • Capital expenditures of $3.6 – $3.8 billion
    • Adjusted effective tax rate of 23%

data.title: Euro Sun Mining signs term sheet for up to $200M facility
data.description:
• Euro Sun Mining (TSX:ESM:CA) has agreed in principle to the terms and conditions for a copper concentrates prepayment facility of up to $200M offered by Trafigura Pte.
  • This facility is aimed at securing the financial requirements needed by the company to complete all necessary permitting and further investment over the next 18 months to advance the Rovina Valley copper-gold project.
  • The lender intends to assist in the syndication of a consortium to provide the company with an additional debt component of up to $200M upon reaching the construction phase of the company’s Rovina Valley Project.

data.title: Regeneron, Sanofi win FDA label expansion for Dupixent against rare skin disorder
data.description:
• Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:REGN) and Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) announced Friday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has approved a label expansion for their asthma therapy Dupixent as a treatment for a rare skin disorder called bullous pemphigoid.
  • The companies said that based on data from their pivotal ADEPT phase 2/3 study, the FDA has approved their blockbuster for adults with BP, a condition characterized by itching, blisters, and other lesions on the skin, mainly among elderly patients.
  • In 2024, Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) and Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) announced that ADEPT met the primary and all key secondary endpoints in patients with moderate-to-severe BP.
  • In February, the FDA accepted their supplemental biologics license application for the agency’s priority review of the drug in BP, granting June 20, 2025, as the target action date. The approval marks Dupixent's eighth U.S.-licensed indication with underlying type 2 inflammation.

data.title: So-Young announces extension of plan to implement ADS ratio change
data.description:
• So-Young International (NASDAQ:SY) on Friday announced that it is amending the effective date for the previously announced plan for an ADS ratio change.

  • The company had previously announced plans to change the ratio of its American depositary shares to its Class A ordinary shares, from thirteen ADSs representing ten Class A ordinary shares to one ADS representing fifteen Class A ordinary shares. 

  • Following further consideration, the Company has decided to take additional time to finalize preparations for the ADS ratio change. An updated timeline will be announced once it becomes available.

data.title: KKR updates on quarter-to-date monetization activity
data.description:
KKR (NYSE:KKR) said on Friday that its asset management unit earned realized performance income and investment income of more than $475M quarter-to-date through June 20.

The amount includes performance income that is expected to be crystallized at June 30.

During Q1, the company had reported total monetization activity of $566M (+40% year-over-year). On March 24, KKR had reported realized performance income and investment income of over $475M for the period from January 1 to March 24.

For the latest update, the monetization activity quarter-to-date in the second quarter is made up of ~75% realized performance income and about 25% realized investment income.
